title: Boat Rental in Cannes: Prices, Seasons and What to Know 2026
html:
Renting a yacht in Cannes means casting off from the Vieux Port, the old harbour beside La Croisette, and steering for the Lérins Islands within minutes. Day rates on BoatHire24 run from roughly EUR 350 for a small motorboat to several thousand for a crewed yacht, depending on size and season.

Where to set off in Cannes

Most charters leave from one of a handful of well-known harbours along this stretch of the Cote d'Azur. Knowing the names helps when you confirm a meeting point with the operator.

  • Vieux Port de Cannes, the central old harbour right below La Croisette and the Palais des Festivals.
  • Port Pierre Canto, the larger marina at the eastern end of the bay, popular with bigger yachts.
  • Golfe-Juan, a calm neighbouring bay a short hop west towards Vallauris.
  • Theoule-sur-Mer, gateway to the red rock coves of the Esterel massif.
  • Ile Sainte-Marguerite and Ile Saint-Honorat, the two Lerins Islands that anchor most day trips.

Which boat should you rent in Cannes?

Choose a small motorboat for couples, a sportscruiser for the Lerins run, or a crewed yacht for a full day with catering and a skipper. The right boat depends on group size, budget and how far you want to roam along the bay.

  1. Small open motorboat: best for couples and short coastal pottering, seats up to 6, from EUR 350 a day.
  2. RIB or sportsboat: best for watersports and reaching Theoule quickly, seats up to 8, from EUR 500 a day.
  3. Day sailing yacht: best for a relaxed island sail, seats up to 8, from EUR 600 a day.
  4. Motor yacht: best for comfort and shade, and a roomy choice like the Mefasa 90 motor yacht suits larger groups; you can read the full spec on the Mefasa 90 listing, seats up to 12, from EUR 2,500 a day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht: best for events and full-day cruising with a skipper, seats up to 12, from EUR 4,000 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes?

Expect anything from about EUR 60 for a shared seat to several thousand for a private crewed yacht over a full day. Prices climb sharply in summer and dip in spring and autumn, and you can compare live rates and book a yacht charter Cannes directly when you have firm dates.

  • A shared group tour starts around EUR 60 per person for a half-day in EUR.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day starts near EUR 250 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day typically runs EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500 in EUR.
  • A crewed luxury yacht commonly begins around EUR 4,000 per day in EUR, fuel extra.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes?

In France a licence is generally required to skipper a motorboat above a modest power threshold, though smaller engines and all crewed charters need none from you. If you have no qualification, hire a boat with a skipper or pick a low-power model.

The high season here peaks from June through September, when the Mistral wind can funnel down the coast and kick up a short chop by midday. Book a morning slot to enjoy the calmest water, and always let the operator confirm the exact licence requirements for the specific boat you want.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 350 to over EUR 4,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: a half-day of 4 hours or a full day of 8 hours
  • Capacity: most day boats carry 6 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Nearest islands: the Lerins, under 2 nautical miles from the Vieux Port

Questions about boat rental in Cannes

How far are the Lerins Islands from Cannes?

The Lerins Islands sit under 2 nautical miles offshore, so Ile Sainte-Marguerite is roughly a fifteen-minute cruise from the Vieux Port, making them the natural first anchorage for almost every day charter from the bay.

What is the best time of year to charter in Cannes?

June to September brings the warmest sea and longest days, while May and October offer milder prices and quieter coves; spring sailing is pleasant, though water temperatures stay cooler until early summer settles in.

Can I charter a yacht in Cannes without sailing experience?

Yes, you can book a crewed charter where a professional skipper handles the boat entirely, leaving you free to relax, swim and enjoy the coastline without holding any qualification or prior sailing background.

How long is a typical day charter in Cannes?

A standard day charter lasts about 8 hours, while half-day options of roughly 4 hours suit a quick island run; both usually include time to anchor, swim and have lunch aboard.

Cannes rewards a day afloat, from the calm of Golfe-Juan to the pines of Saint-Honorat, and in 2026 it remains one of the easiest Riviera bases to plan around. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cannes or list your own, with verified boats across hundreds of destinations.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cannes and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
